methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $180M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
AMP Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $180M+, is valued against a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable in the robotics sector. The peer set ranges from Series B through Series D, with recent valuations spanning $300M to $10.0B, including established players like Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), and 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $180M raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $306M. The peer-median method calculates the median valuation across the nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables at $1.6B, with the peer range establishing bounds from $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated analysis implies a valuation range of $260M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $953M. This wide spread reflects material divergence between stage-based progression and peer-set positioning. The stage method underweights peer valuations in a frontier-tech sector where similar-stage robotics companies command substantial multiples on capital raised. Key uncertainties include AMP's specific traction relative to Hadrian and other Series C peers, the relevance of higher-stage comps (Exotec, 1X), and sector momentum sustainability. Further diligence on revenue, unit economics, and customer concentration is required to narrow the range.
